2006-05-01.title: Bolivia Nationalizes Natural Gas Industry
2006-05-01.abstract: The move by President Evo Morales highlights Latin America's struggle over control of energy resources.

2006-05-01.title: Debate Over Condoms and AIDS Tests the Pope
2006-05-01.abstract: At the Vatican, a clash is shaping up between the church's ban on condom use and its advocacy of human life.

2006-05-01.title: Egypt Renews Emergency Detention Law
2006-05-01.abstract: For 25 years the measure has effectively allowed the Egyptian government to detain prisoners indefinitely and without charge.

2006-05-01.title: Deadline Passes Without Darfur Accord
2006-05-01.abstract: The talks were extended 48 hours after Sudan's government accepted a peace agreement, but two of Darfur's three main rebel groups raised last-minute objections.

2006-05-01.title: Mock Iraqi Villages in Mojave Prepare Troops for Battle
2006-05-01.abstract: The training villages are at the heart of a vast shift in U.S. strategy, from an emphasis on army-to-army battles to preparing for guerrilla insurgencies.

2006-05-02.title: Iran Urges United Nations to Oppose American 'Threats'
2006-05-02.abstract: Iran asked the U.N. to take a stand against threats that included possible nuclear strikes on its territory and that were "in total contempt of international law."

2006-05-02.title: How Bad Is Inflation in Zimbabwe?
2006-05-02.abstract: Prices have increased 900%, with toilet paper costing $417 per sheet at one supermarket.

2006-05-02.title: U.S.A. Is No. 1, on London List of Traffic Fees
2006-05-02.abstract: The U.S. leads the list of countries whose diplomats here have repeatedly failed to pay the $14 congestion charge levied on motorists entering central London.

2006-05-02.title: Italian Premier Quits After Defying Vote
2006-05-02.abstract: Silvio Berlusconi's resignation will start the countdown to the formation of a new government.

2006-05-02.title: Bush Calls Sudan's Leader About Darfur Talks
2006-05-02.abstract: President Bush called Omar al-Bashir today, urging him to send a representative back to peace talks on Darfur.

2006-05-03.title: Europe Cancels Talks With Serbia Over Fugitive
2006-05-03.abstract: The surrender of General Ratko Mladic is a condition for talks about Serbian membership in the European Union.

2006-05-03.title: Another Bishop Is Installed in China
2006-05-03.abstract: The move could derail diplomatic efforts aimed at the re-establishing diplomatic relations between Beijing and the Holy See.

2006-05-03.title: World Briefing: Asia, Africa, Middle East and Europe
De Villepin Decries Vilification of His Character
2006-05-03.abstract: Prime Minister Dominique de Villepin called for an end to the "lies and calumny" that have cast him as a spiteful bureaucrat.

2006-05-03.title: Taliban Threat Is Said to Grow in Afghan South
2006-05-03.abstract: The Taliban, counting on the imminent departure of U.S. troops, appear to be increasing their insurgency in southern Afghanistan.

2006-05-04.title: Video: Russia Rebuilds Grozny
2006-05-04.abstract: Kyle Crichton of the Foreign desk discusses the current rebuilding efforts in Grozny, Chechnya's capital.

2006-05-04.title: In New Problem for Palestinians, Banks Reject Transfers
2006-05-04.abstract: Arab countries have raised more than $70 million, but banks are afraid to move the money to the West Bank or the Gaza Strip.

2006-05-04.title: In Aftermath of Plane Crash, Grief and Speculation Abound
2006-05-04.abstract: The crash of an Armenian airliner in southern Russia, which killed all 113 aboard, led the newspapers.

2006-05-04.title: An Incomplete Peace: Sudan's Never-Ending War With Itself
2006-05-04.abstract: When the civil war in southern Sudan ended in 2005, the West hoped Darfur would also become peaceful.  Instead, the conflict there has escalated.

2006-05-04.title: Under U.S. Pressure, Mexico President Seeks Review of Drug Law
2006-05-04.abstract: President Vicente Fox has asked Congress to reconsider a law that would decriminalize possession of small amounts of drugs.

2006-05-05.title:
Cheney Rebukes 
Russia on 
Rights
2006-05-05.abstract: The vice president's comments appeared to lay down new markers for a relationship that has become increasingly strained.

2006-05-05.title: Millions Vote in Local Races in Britain, Testing Blair
2006-05-05.abstract: The ballot, the first test of public opinion since the national vote a year ago, is likely to lead to a rearrangement of the prime minister's cabinet.

2006-05-05.title: U.S. Explains Itself to U.N. on Torture Charges
2006-05-05.abstract: The U.S. denied that the it systematically mistreated prisoners and reiterated a commitment to a ban on torture.

2006-05-05.title: Razing of Muslim Shrine in India Brings Violence, and a Court Ban
2006-05-05.abstract: Six people died and dozens were injured this week during protests against the demolition of a Muslim shrine in Gujarat.

2006-05-06.title: Mexican Mine Disaster Brings Charges of Collusion
2006-05-06.abstract: Fallout from a mine tragedy in a remote corner of northern Mexico has set off one of the largest labor crises in the country's recent history.

2006-05-06.title: Not All See Video Mockery of Zarqawi as Good Strategy
2006-05-06.abstract: Military officers questioned whether outtakes of the Al Qaeda leader in Iraq were convincing in suggesting his incompetence.

2006-05-06.title: A Suicide Bomb, a Dead Daughter and a Test of Faith
2006-05-06.abstract: Within hours of the suicide bombings in London last summer, Julie Nicholson knew, but could not at first accept, that she had lost her 24-year-old daughter.

2006-05-06.title: Palestinian Asks Israeli Premier to Begin Talks
2006-05-06.abstract: Israel's prime minister is expected to meet with the Palestinian president after he returns from Washington.

2006-05-06.title: Stung by Election Losses, Blair Reshuffles Cabinet
2006-05-06.abstract: The most unexpected of the high-profile casualties was Jack Straw, the foreign secretary.

2006-05-07.title: China Ordains Another Bishop
2006-05-07.abstract: Another Catholic bishop was consecrated in China today, in a ceremony that was approved by the Vatican.

2006-05-07.title: Out of Desert Poverty, a Caldron of Rage in the Sinai
2006-05-07.abstract: A tumble-down Egyptian village in the Northern Sinai, where Bedouin culture has been marginalized, has become a caldron of Islamic rage.

2006-05-07.title: Gypsies Gain a Legal Tool in Rights Fight
2006-05-07.abstract: Using the civil rights struggle of black Americans as a model, the Gypsies, or Roma, are filing lawsuits under new antidiscrimination laws in Eastern Europe.

2006-05-07.title: 10 U.S. Soldiers Are Killed in Afghan Helicopter Crash
2006-05-07.abstract: The soldiers were among 2,500 coalition and Afghan forces taking part in an attack on insurgents.

2006-05-07.title: Vietnam's Deep-Rooted Corruption Threatens Development
2006-05-07.abstract: The discovery of $7 million worth of bets on soccer games set investigators on a trail that led to the resignation of one official and the jailing of his deputy.

2006-05-08.title: Israeli Police Oust Hebron Jewish Settlers
2006-05-08.abstract: The case was seen as a test for Prime Minister Ehud Olmert, who had said that Israel would not tolerate lawlessness by Jewish settlers.

2006-05-08.title: China Installs Bishop With Approval of Vatican
2006-05-08.abstract: Another Catholic bishop was consecrated in China Sunday in a ceremony approved by the Vatican but unlikely to ease tensions.

2006-05-08.title: Blair Again Dismisses Calls for an Early Departure
2006-05-08.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair today dismissed calls by dissidents within his ruling Labor Party to set a date for his departure.

2006-05-08.title: Aid Workers Exploit Liberian Girls, Report Says
2006-05-08.abstract: Liberian girls are being sexually exploited by United Nations peacekeepers, aid workers and teachers, according to a new report.

2006-05-08.title: Battle Rages Over Future of Labor Party After Blair
2006-05-08.abstract: Britain's ruling Labor Party finished third in Thursday's local elections, and Labor dissidents are pressing for a timetable to elect a new leader.

2006-05-09.title: Key Iraqi Posts to Go to Independent Officials
2006-05-09.abstract: Iraq's prime minister designate said that new ministers of defense and interior would have no ties to politics or militias.

2006-05-09.title: Killings in Iraq Spawn Search for Missing Funds
2006-05-09.abstract: The investigation of a slain human rights worker has widened to include the whereabouts of hundreds of thousands of U.S. dollars.

2006-05-09.title: Aid Workers Are Said to Abuse Girls
2006-05-09.abstract: Liberian girls are being sexually exploited by United Nations peacekeepers, aid workers and teachers, according to a new report.

2006-05-09.title: India's Boy Marathoner Runs Into Critics
2006-05-09.abstract: A human rights group is investigating whether running a young boy in a 40-mile race constituted child abuse.

2006-05-09.title: Iranian President's Letter to Bush Emerges
2006-05-09.abstract: The letter did not address directly the central issue that divides the two countries: Iran's nuclear ambitions.

2006-05-10.title: How to Help Struggling Palestinians
2006-05-10.abstract: The Times's correspondent Steven R. Weisman reports from the UN that the European Union and the United States differ on how to aid the Palestinian people. Producer: Emily Hager

2006-05-10.title: Bill to Double U.S. Funds for Child Survival
2006-05-10.abstract: The bill would double the $356 million the U.S.A.I.D. spends to improve the prospects of mothers and children in developing countries.

2006-05-10.title: Bush Says Iranian's Letter Misses the Point
2006-05-10.abstract: President Bush said President Ahmadinejad's letter failed to say when Iran would "get rid of" its nuclear program.

2006-05-10.title: China Not Manipulating Currency, U.S. Says
2006-05-10.abstract: The finding is likely to prompt a strong response from groups that consider China's trading practices unfair.

2006-05-10.title: Iranian Letter: Using Religion to Lecture Bush
2006-05-10.abstract: Iran's president wrote to President Bush that the invasion of Iraq could not be reconciled with Christian values.

2006-05-11.title: Kyrgyzstan: Crime Boss-Lawmaker Killed
2006-05-11.abstract: Rysbek Akmatbayev, said to be an organized-crime boss and recently elected to Parliament, was killed in Bishkek, the capital. He was shot 30 times leaving a mosque after evening prayers, the authorities said. He had earlier in the day appeared in court to face charges in the 2004 killing of a policeman. Although he was elected to Parliament by a sweeping majority last month, the government, under heavy international pressure, refused to seat Mr. Akmatbayev because of criminal charges against him.

2006-05-11.title: As Gazans Wait for Aid, Their Situation Is Dire
2006-05-11.abstract: Palestinian Authority employees, 35 percent of the population, have not been paid in two months, and tensions in Gaza are rising fast.

2006-05-11.title: Putin Urges Plan to Reverse Slide in the Birth Rate
2006-05-11.abstract: The Russian president directed Parliament to adopt a 10-year program that would extend maternity leave and subsidize day care.

2006-05-11.title: Squid and Text Messages Save 3
2006-05-11.abstract: John Tabo and his son and nephew survived for 22 days on raw squid and rainwater as their small boat drifted in choppy waters off the northeastern tip of Australia, the police said. When their boat moved back within range of cellphone service, they were located. A police official said the trio had switched off their cellphones for extended periods to save the batteries, until they had enough coverage. Then they started sending text messages back to family members of where they thought they were.

2006-05-11.title: Nigeria: American Oil Worker Shot to Death
2006-05-11.abstract: A gunman on a motorcycle shot and killed an American oil worker in Port Harcourt, in the southern Niger Delta oil region, the police said. The American worked for the Houston-based drilling equipment maker Baker Hughes and was riding in a car to his office when he was killed. The police had no further details. A Baker Hughes spokesman declined to identify the employee until his family was notified. A militant group called the Movement for the Emancipation of the Niger Delta has been fighting for local control of oil revenues and has kidnapped American and other foreign oil workers and released them later unharmed, but said it was not responsible for the killing yesterday.

2006-05-12.title: Universities in Crisis
2006-05-12.abstract: The value of a bachelor's degree is plummeting in France's archaic state-owned university system. (Producer: Emily B. Hager)

2006-05-12.title: Jordan Islamists Stir Tensions by Displaying Election Skills
2006-05-12.abstract: Jordan's Islamists have raised their sights, preparing to take part in possible council elections this year and parliamentary elections next year.

2006-05-12.title: Police Beat Crowds Backing Egypt's Judges
2006-05-12.abstract: The demonstrators were intent on showing support for judges demanding independence from President Hosni Mubarak.

2006-05-12.title: Colombian Court Legalizes Some Abortions
2006-05-12.abstract: The decision is expected to embolden women's rights groups across Latin America to use courts to try to roll back some of the world's most stringent abortion laws.

2006-05-12.title: China's Exports Climbed Steeply in April
2006-05-12.abstract: The unexpectedly wide trade surplus is likely to further upset China's critics in the U.S., Europe and Japan.

2006-05-13.title: 6 Weeks After Ukrainian Vote, It's Unclear Who Won
2006-05-13.abstract: The politicians trying to reunite the political parties that were swept to power a year and a half ago are divided on who will be Ukraine's new prime minister.

2006-05-13.title: Mexico: Baby's Sale on Internet Is Thwarted
2006-05-13.abstract: A Mexican man and woman were arrested in Nuevo Laredo on charges that they had tried to sell a four-week-old baby over the Internet. The transaction came to light when a Dallas man learned of the offer in a chatroom and alerted reporters at a Univision television station, who contacted the authorities. Mexican and American officials set up a sting operation and on Thursday arrested the couple, Ana Luis Hidalgo-Rivera and Alex Hernández of Monterrey, as they tried to exchange the infant for $50,000.

2006-05-13.title: Zimbabwe: Inflation Tops 1,000 Percent
2006-05-13.abstract: Zimbabwe's annual inflation rate climbed to 1,042.9 percent in April, a record that surpassed the 914 percent rate recorded in March, the government reported. The Central Statistical Office had unexpectedly delayed for two days the release of the monthly tally, which has risen inexorably for a year. The rate in June 2005 was 164 percent. Citizens have generally borne the burden quietly, with exceptions: in Bindura, a northern town, the police arrested 39 university students after a campus building housing computer equipment was set on fire, state television reported. The fire apparently was set to protest huge increases in school fees.

2006-05-13.title: Nepal Arrests 5 Who Served as Officials Under King
2006-05-13.abstract: The decision came roughly three weeks after King Gyanendra, ceded control of the government to an elected Parliament that was suspended in 2002.

2006-05-13.title: U.N. Finds New Uranium Traces in Iran
2006-05-13.abstract: The findings raise new questions about whether Iran harbors a clandestine nuclear program.

2006-05-14.title: Despite Political Pressure to Scale Back, Logistics Are Pinning Down U.S. in Iraq
2006-05-14.abstract: Daunting challenges beyond the volatile security situation in Iraq are preventing a rapid withdrawal of American troops.

2006-05-14.title: Avian Flu Wanes in Asian Nations It First Hit Hard
2006-05-14.abstract: Even as it crops up in Europe and Africa, the bird flu that raised fears of a human pandemic has been largely snuffed out in Southeast Asia.

2006-05-14.title: 2 Car Bombs Kill 14 Outside Baghdad Airport
2006-05-14.abstract: The attack was the deadliest in a day of violence that left 32 people dead, including two American soldiers.

2006-05-14.title: U.S. Aid Can't Win Bolivia's Love as New Suitors Emerge
2006-05-14.abstract: To the Bush administration's dismay, Bolivia is forming closer bonds with Cuba and Venezuela, thanks to their development aid.

2006-05-14.title: Italy's Once-Plucky Little Factories Now Complicate Its Battle With 'Made in China'
2006-05-14.abstract: Once the secret behind Italy's success, the small family factory appears ill-suited to global competition.

2006-05-15.title: Europe Tries to Restrain Iran's Atomic Ambitions
2006-05-15.abstract: The European Union said today that it would offer an incentive package to Tehran, including technology to build nuclear power plants for civilian energy production.

2006-05-15.title: Canada Miner Raises Bid
2006-05-15.abstract: Inco, the Canadian nickel miner, increased its offer for Falconbridge, a Toronto-based competitor, to 19.6 billion Canadian dollars.

2006-05-15.title: Hussein Is Formally Charged for War Crimes
2006-05-15.abstract:
Saddam Hussein refused to enter a plea and 
insisted that he remained Iraq's rightful president.

2006-05-15.title: Sri Lankan City Mired in Ethnic Violence
2006-05-15.abstract: Recent violence in Trincomalee harkens back to 1983's anti-Tamil pogrom that plunged the country into  war.

2006-05-15.title: Israel Raids West Bank Towns, Killing 6
2006-05-15.abstract: An Islamic Jihad leader accused of orchestrating the April 17 suicide bombing of a Tel Aviv restaurant was among the dead.

2006-05-16.title: Malawi: Vice President Under House Arrest
2006-05-16.abstract: A court placed Vice President Cassim Chilumpha under house arrest on charges of plotting to kill the president, Bingu wa Mutharika. The government says Mr. Chilumpha and two businessmen planned to hire South African killers for the assassination. Mr. Chilumpha says he is being persecuted for refusing to join the president's party.

2006-05-16.title: A Critic of Muslim Intolerance Faces Loss of Dutch Citizenship
2006-05-16.abstract: The Dutch government on Monday abruptly threatened to revoke the citizenship of one of the country's most prominent members of Parliament.

2006-05-16.title: Northern Ireland: Mothballed Assembly Meets
2006-05-16.abstract: Political parties met in Belfast for the first time since the Northern Ireland Assembly, the Protestant and Roman Catholic provincial government, was suspended three and a half years ago. The occasion was the start of a new effort to revive the assembly. The British and Irish governments have set a November deadline for members to set up a new power-sharing executive or have their $60,000 annual salaries canceled.

2006-05-16.title: Egypt: Mubarak's Son Meets Bush and Cheney
2006-05-16.abstract: Gamal Mubarak, the son of President Hosni Mubarak and his presumed political heir, talked briefly on Friday with President Bush while at the White House. Mr. Bush saw Mr. Mubarak while he spoke with Stephen J. Hadley, the national security adviser, said Frederick Jones, a National Security Council spokesman. Mr. Mubarak also met with Vice President Dick Cheney. Neither Mr. Jones nor Mr. Cheney's office would further discuss the substance of the meetings, which came amid increasing criticism from the Bush administration of the Mubarak government for its crackdown on the opposition.

2006-05-16.title: China Supports Europe's Incentives Plan for Iran
2006-05-16.abstract: China said that it supported Europe's approach to resolving the standoff over Iran's nuclear program.

2006-05-17.title: 5 Days of Violence by Gangs in São Paulo Leaves 115 Dead Before Subsiding
2006-05-17.abstract: The scale of the fighting has prompted many to question an already shaky faith in Brazil's public security forces.

2006-05-17.title: Iraqi Parliament Session Is Called for Cabinet
2006-05-17.abstract: Iraq's Prime Minister-designate called today for Parliament to meet on Saturday to ratify his Cabinet.

2006-05-17.title: New Security Force Widens Palestinian  Rift
2006-05-17.abstract: The Hamas-led Palestinian government's new force is a direct challenge to President Mahmoud Abbas.

2006-05-17.title: Obstacles Test African Force in Grim Darfur
2006-05-17.abstract: The African Union force in Darfur has been hamstrung by inadequate equipment, too few troops and a limited mandate.

2006-05-17.title: Canada: Military Recruitment Falling Short
2006-05-17.abstract: The military is barely recruiting enough newcomers to replace its retiring veterans, according to a report by the government's auditor general. In the last four years, the military recruited 20,000 people, but the effort resulted in a net gain of just 700 fully trained troops. The report — which comes after the government said it would increase the armed forces by 23,000 full- and part-time members over five years — said the government would have to change the way it attracted and trained recruits if it expected to bolster those ranks.

2006-05-18.title: U.S. to Turn Over 16 Saudis From Guantánamo to Riyadh
2006-05-18.abstract: The decision came after months of wrangling over American demands that the men be treated humanely once in Saudi custody.

2006-05-18.title: Russia: Orthodox Official to Meet Pope
2006-05-18.abstract: A senior official of the Russian Orthodox Church, Metropolitan Kirill, is to meet Pope Benedict XVI at the Vatican today in the latest of a series of steps signaling a warming of relations with the Roman Catholic Church. Metropolitan Kirill, who serves as the church's foreign minister, met Benedict privately shortly after he became pope last year, but this is to be an official visit, a spokesman, Archpriest Vsevold Chaplin, said in a telephone interview.

2006-05-18.title: Brazil: São Paulo Police Kill 22 Suspected Gangsters
2006-05-18.abstract: The police in São Paulo said that overnight they had killed at least 22 suspected members of an organized crime group that opened a violent offensive in the city on Friday. As the police continued to hunt down suspects with sweeps through slums and random checks on motorists, a São Paulo-based rights group, the Social Network for Justice and Human Rights, suggested that they were engaged in a

2006-05-18.title: Hamas-Led Government Deploys Security Force, Defying Abbas
2006-05-18.abstract: The deployment is a direct challenge to President Mahmoud Abbas's authority. He vetoed the creation of the force last month.

2006-05-18.title: Sudden Rise of Violence in Afghanistan
2006-05-18.abstract: A U.S. narcotics agent was killed in the west and fighting in the south left possibly dozens of Taliban fighters dead.

2006-05-19.title: Pakistan: Musharraf Hints at Another Term
2006-05-19.abstract: President Pervez Musharraf publicly indicated that he might seek another term, saying the current Parliament and provincial legislatures could re-elect him before his term expires on Nov. 15, 2007, and they are dissolved for new elections and replaced. In a television interview, he said a new presidential election could legally be held two months before his term ended. Under the Constitution, Parliament and the provincial legislatures elect a president.

2006-05-19.title:
U.N. Panel 
Backs Closing 
Guantánamo
2006-05-19.abstract: The panel's report came on the same day as American military officers detailed a melee at the detention center.

2006-05-19.title: As Death Stalks Iraq, Middle-Class Exodus Begins
2006-05-19.abstract: Increasing portions of the middle class seem to be doing everything they can to leave Iraq.

2006-05-19.title: Trade Minister Presses Effort to Battle Extortion of Businesses
2006-05-19.abstract: Russia's minister of trade and economic development rebuked law enforcement agencies for extorting money from private business.

2006-05-19.title: Inquiry Implies Civilian Deaths in Iraq Topped Initial Report
2006-05-19.abstract: The inquiry, which was still weeks from completion, has already raised fundamental questions about the propriety of actions by the troops, and official said.

2006-05-20.title: Vatican Disciplines Founder of Order Over Abuse Charges
2006-05-20.abstract: The founder of the Legionaries of Christ was asked to give up his public ministry in favor of a quiet life of "prayer and penitence."

2006-05-20.title: Iraqis Name Cabinet Today but Security Ministers Later
2006-05-20.abstract: Iraqi political leaders still have not agreed on candidates for the two most important cabinet posts, but will go ahead with presenting a new government.

2006-05-20.title: In Word Feud With 'Hitler,' 'Satan' Draws Line in Sand
2006-05-20.abstract: Already poor relations worsened when the Bush administration announced on Monday that the U.S. would sell no more military equipment to Venezuela.

2006-05-20.title: Seeking United Latin America, Venezuela's Chávez Is a Divider
2006-05-20.abstract: Some of Venezuela's neighbors have expressed rising impatience at what they see as Hugo Chávez's meddling.

2006-05-20.title: Court Overrules Parts of Law Shielding Colombia's Warlords
2006-05-20.abstract: The ruling drew a swift, threatening response from the paramilitary leaders, who live on ranches in northern Colombia.

2006-05-21.title: Iraqis Form Government, With Crucial Posts Vacant
2006-05-21.abstract: The approved full-term government appeared to lack the cohesion needed to quell sectarian and guerrilla violence.

2006-05-21.title: As New Leaders Seek Unity, Fresh Attacks Deepen Rifts
2006-05-21.abstract: A bombing that killed 19 people on Saturday underscored the depth of the challenge that the new Iraqi government is facing.

2006-05-21.title: Leader of Italy's Most Revered Soccer Club Takes a Spill
2006-05-21.abstract: Luciano Moggi is alleged to have indirectly fixed games by manipulating refereeing assignments to the benefit of certain teams.

2006-05-21.title: Quotas to Aid India's Poor Spark Push for Meritocracy
2006-05-21.abstract: A proposal to help more students of lower castes attend the country's most competitive universities has sparked nationwide protests.

2006-05-21.title: Palestinian Leader Meets With Israeli Foreign Minister
2006-05-21.abstract: The officials met in the highest-level contacts between the two sides since Hamas won the Palestinian elections.

2006-05-22.title: On a Violent Day, Iraq's New Leader Unveils Ideas for Tackling Security Challenges
2006-05-22.abstract: On Sunday, Iraq's prime minister promised to confront terrorists and killers, but 20 deaths showed he has a tough job ahead.

2006-05-22.title: Israeli Foreign Minister and Abbas Meet at Economic Forum
2006-05-22.abstract: The meeting was the highest-level contact between Israel and the Palestinians since Hamas won January's Palestinian elections.

2006-05-22.title: Afghan Parliament Rejects Budget Over Teacher Raises
2006-05-22.abstract: Lawmakers said that spending was not evenly distributed around the country and that the budget was unfair to civil servants, who earn $40 a month.

2006-05-22.title: Kuwait's Emir Calls Election, Citing 'Stability'
2006-05-22.abstract: Kuwait's emir dissolved the country's Parliament on Sunday, ending a battle over redistricting that threatened to cause a governmental crisis.

2006-05-22.title: U.S. Assault on Taliban Kills Afghan Villagers
2006-05-22.abstract: A U.S.-led bombing that killed at least 20 Taliban fighters also left 16 civilians dead, according to local sources.

2006-05-23.title: Pentagon Questions China's Military Aims
2006-05-23.abstract: China's leadership has not satisfactorily explained its military expansion, according to a new report.

2006-05-23.title: Israel Says It Arrested a Hamas Militant
2006-05-23.abstract: Ibrahim Hamed orchestrated several of the deadliest suicide bombings in recent years, the Israeli military said.

2006-05-23.title: Quotas to Aid India's Poor vs. Push for Meritocracy
2006-05-23.abstract: Protests have spread across the country after the government announced a plan to extend college admission quotas to certain "backward" castes.

2006-05-23.title: Afghan Leader Orders Inquiry Into U.S. Bombing
2006-05-23.abstract: The bombardment by American planes of an Afghan village killed at least 16 civilians.

2006-05-23.title: Sharp Drop in Stock Exchange Ignites Concern on Economy
2006-05-23.abstract: Russia's stock exchange plunged nine percent on Monday in panic selling that affected developing markets around the world.

2006-05-24.title: Religion Returns to Chechnya
2006-05-24.abstract: The Times's C.J. Chivers reports on the revival of a dance ritual among  Chechnya's Sufi Muslims, a sect that is also erecting the region's largest mosque.    Producer: Adam B. Ellick

2006-05-24.title: Tariq Aziz Sings Praises of Hussein at His Trial
2006-05-24.abstract: The tirelessly reverential former foreign minister relentlessly defended his old boss on the stand today.

2006-05-24.title: Gun Battle Erupts During Israeli Raid
2006-05-24.abstract: An Israeli undercover arrest operation turned into a chaotic gun battle today, leaving four Palestinians dead and about 50 injured.

2006-05-24.title: U.S. Plan to Lure Nurses May Hurt Poor Nations
2006-05-24.abstract: The Senate immigration bill would throw open the gate to nurses and, some fear, drain them from developing countries.

2006-05-24.title: W.H.O. Seeks to Speed Choice of a New Leader
2006-05-24.abstract: After Dr. Lee Jong Wook died on Monday, it became apparent that the U.N. agency did not have a swift way to choose a successor.

2006-05-25.title: Palestinian Leader Plans a Vote on Accepting Israel
2006-05-25.abstract: President Mahmoud Abbas today proposed a referendum on a Palestinian state that would exist alongside Israel.

2006-05-25.title: Australian Forces Arrive in East Timor to Quell Violence
2006-05-25.abstract: East Timor's foreign minister, José Ramos-Horta, appealed for outside help to disarm "renegade troops and police rebelling against the state."

2006-05-25.title: On a Paper Border, Mexico's Poor Hide, Scramble and Hope
2006-05-25.abstract: President Vicente Fox was not the only Mexican citizen traveling to Washington State on Wednesday.

2006-05-25.title: Russia: Think Dollars, Say Rubles
2006-05-25.abstract: The dollar is still king in Russia's economy, but it may soon disappear from the lips of the country's officials. The lower house of Parliament gave preliminary approval to a bill that would bar officials from citing dollars — or euros — in any public statements or media interviews. The significance appears largely psychological, but the bill's supporters consider it an important step to bolster the country's currency, the ruble. Russian law bans transactions in any foreign currency, but prices for all manner of goods — from apartments to cars — are routinely discussed and advertised in dollars.

2006-05-25.title: Israeli Raid in West Bank Kills 4 Palestinians
2006-05-25.abstract: An Israeli undercover operation turned into a chaotic gun battle Wednesday, leaving four Palestinians dead and about 50 injured.

2006-05-26.title: Covering a Friend's Back: Leaders Reverse the Roles
2006-05-26.abstract: Tony Blair has always served as the world's Bush-whisperer, but at their joint news conference last night, it was almost the reverse.

2006-05-26.title: Taiwan: President's In-Law Charged With Insider Trading
2006-05-26.abstract: Prosecutors said President Chen Shui-bian's son-in-law, Chao Chien-min, had been detained on suspicion of insider trading. Investigators spent hours questioning Mr. Chao and his parents, brother and sister-in-law on Wednesday about the purchase of shares in the ailing Taiwan Development Corporation. The value of the stock skyrocketed shortly after the purchase was made.

2006-05-26.title: Bush and Blair Concede Errors, but Defend War
2006-05-26.abstract: The prime minister of Britain said that instead of wanting foreign troops to be withdrawn from Iraq, other nations needed to show more support for the effort to bring democracy to Iraq.

2006-05-26.title: Urgent Health Care Effort Takes Shape
2006-05-26.abstract: Thirty-six of the 57 countries with the severest shortages of health workers are in Africa — and those 57 countries together need four million more doctors, nurses, midwives and other workers, the World Health Organization estimates. The agency announced that Canada, Norway, the European Commission, the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation and the International Council of Nurses would join in trying to rapidly increase the number of workers by expanding the ranks of teachers and the capacity of educational institutions.

2006-05-26.title: Here Comes the Bride (but Not From Afar, Emirates Hope)
2006-05-26.abstract: In this nation of five million, of whom only about 10 percent are native citizens, the battle for cultural survival begins with "I do."

2006-05-27.title: Abbas Enlists Prisoners to Unsettle Hamas
2006-05-27.abstract: By siding with Palestinian prisoners, Palestinian Prime Minister Mahmoud Abbas is hoping to push Hamas to recognize Israel.

2006-05-27.title: Russia: Moscow Court Bars Gay Parade
2006-05-27.abstract: A Moscow court upheld a decision by Mayor Yuri M. Luzhkov to refuse permission to hold a gay-rights parade in the city today. Plans for the parade, which would have been the city's first, provoked a storm of condemnation in Russia, where homosexuality has gained little acceptance since it a criminal ban on it was lifted in 1993. In remarks yesterday, Mr. Luzhkov called homosexuality

2006-05-27.title: Australian Forces in Timor Capital to Deter Warring Sides
2006-05-27.abstract: Australian troops patrolled East Timor's capital, Dili, on Friday, but were not yet in control of the warring factions in the city.

2006-05-27.title: Fugitives From Somali Capital Describe Horrors of War
2006-05-27.abstract: Evacuees from Mogadishu dream of artillery shells exploding around them, and cannot get the sound of gunfire out of their heads.

2006-05-27.title: Great Pyramid as Cuckoo Clock? It Might Not Be Crazy
2006-05-27.abstract: Jean-Pierre Houdin thinks he has calculated how ancient Egyptians assembled pyramids from stones that weighed several tons.

2006-05-28.title: Iran Chief Eclipses Power of Clerics
2006-05-28.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad's consolidation of power presents difficult diplomatic choices for the United States.

2006-05-28.title: Sri Lanka Peace Is in Peril, Norwegian Envoy Warns
2006-05-28.abstract: A top Norwegian envoy warned that Sri Lanka could be heading for war, even as the warring parties agreed to meet to discuss the safety of cease-fire monitors.

2006-05-28.title: East Timor's Capital Spirals Into Violence
2006-05-28.abstract: Australian and Malaysian peacekeepers have been unable to stop fighting between rival gangs.

2006-05-28.title: Afghan Parliament Rejects Chief Justice Nominee
2006-05-28.abstract: Unhappy with widespread judicial corruption, the Parliament rejected the president's nomination of an Islamist cleric for chief of the Supreme Court.

2006-05-28.title: Shunned, Women With H.I.V. Join Forces in Vietnam
2006-05-28.abstract: In the face of fierce discrimination and a lack of health care, women with AIDS in Vietnam are banding together for support.

2006-05-29.title: A German Pope Confronts the Nazi Past at Auschwitz
2006-05-29.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI called the visit "particularly difficult and troubling for a Christian, for a pope from Germany."

2006-05-29.title: Ethnic Tensions Over Cartoon Set Off Riots in Northwest Iran
2006-05-29.abstract: Four were killed and 70 were injured in riots caused by the May 19 publication of a cartoon belittling Iran's Azeri minority.

2006-05-29.title: Bush Ally Coasts to 2nd Term in Colombia
2006-05-29.abstract: Voters were apparently satisfied that President Álvaro Uribe had made headway in wresting control from Marxist rebels and drug traffickers.

2006-05-29.title: Afghans Riot After Deadly Crash by U.S. Military Truck
2006-05-29.abstract: An accident caused by a U.S. military convoy escalated into a full-blown anti-American riot that left at least 14 people dead in Kabul.

2006-05-29.title: New Calls for Inquiry on Handling of Beslan Incident
2006-05-29.abstract: The sole terrorist in Russian custody from the school siege in Beslan in 2004 was sentenced on Friday to life imprisonment, dominating headlines and stirring debate in most newspapers.

2006-05-30.title: Aid Reaches Indonesian Quake Area, but Volcano's Activity Increases
2006-05-30.abstract: A volcano destabilized by Saturday's earthquake could pose a new risk to residents trying to recover.

2006-05-30.title: 3 Dead in Israeli Helicopter Attack in Gaza
2006-05-30.abstract: An Israeli helicopter fired a missile at Palestinian militants during a cross-border clash in northern Gaza, killing three and wounding four, Palestinian officials said. Islamic Jihad said the militants involved were its members. The Israeli military said the militants were about to launch rockets at Israel. Israeli forces also said they had foiled a suicide bombing, capturing two Palestinians carrying a bag with a bomb.

2006-05-30.title: Volkswagen Deal Signals Competition in Auto Market
2006-05-30.abstract: Volkswagen concluded a deal with the Ministry of Economic Development and Trade to open a factory in Russia, an agreement widely covered in newspapers on Tuesday as a sign of new and growing foreign investment.

2006-05-30.title: Israelis Enter Gaza, Kill 4 to Prevent Rocket Attack
2006-05-30.abstract: In its first major incursion into Gaza since last year's pullout, Israel tried a new tactic in its attempts to halt Palestinian rocket fire.

2006-05-30.title: Anti-U.S. Rioting Erupts in Kabul; at Least 14 Dead
2006-05-30.abstract: A deadly accident caused by a U.S. military convoy quickly touched off a full-blown anti-American riot.

2006-05-31.title: U.S. Investigating the Shooting of Two Iraqi Women
2006-05-31.abstract: The women were apparently shot by coalition forces when their car failed to stop at an observation post.

2006-05-31.title:
As Darfur War Rages On,
Disease and Hunger Kill
2006-05-31.abstract: These days most people in Darfur die not from bullets, but from not being able to get health care, clean water or food.

2006-05-31.title: Greece: Culture Aide Survives Bomb Attack
2006-05-31.abstract: Culture Minister George Voulgarakis escaped a terrorist attack when a powerful bomb exploded near his home, in central Athens, wrecking a string of parked cars but causing no injuries. There was no immediate claim of responsibility, but police and security officials said the bombing bore the hallmarks of the Revolutionary Struggle, a band of militant extremists active since 2003, when the police cracked down on Greece's most deadly terrorist group, November 17. Mr. Voulgarakis, 46, who was until February the minister for security, said he been threatened in recent weeks for his alleged role in the illegal interrogation of a group of Pakistanis last year.

2006-05-31.title: After Riots End, Kabul's Residents Begin to Point Fingers
2006-05-31.abstract: Afghans placed blame for Monday's rioting on young hoodlums and criminal gangs, but also criticized the U.S. military's arrogance.

2006-05-31.title: Bush Is Troubled by Haditha Reports
2006-05-31.abstract: If laws were broken, the president said, "there will be punishment," the president said.
